title: T-090 — UI: FactorySpec+BOM bundle approval panel/modal
prompt:
# T-090 — UI: FactorySpec+BOM bundle approval panel/modal

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §0, §5, §17, prototypes/design/03-TECHNICAL-UX.md
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira
Prototype: `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/technical/spec-driven-screens.jsx:653-781` (factory_spec_bom_bundle_approval_modal)

## Goal
UI: FactorySpec+BOM bundle approval panel/modal

UI-only split from T-080. Render a release-bundle panel on BOM and factory_specs detail surfaces. Consume T-080 actions and T-081 adapter; do not implement backend release logic here. Must show paired statuses, blockers from RM usability/supplier_specs/release guards, Technical RBAC/preflight, local D365-disabled messaging, clone-on-write warning, approval/rejection history and audit/evidence link. Use prototype structure/patterns where applicable but red-line obsolete domain copy.

Additional acceptance detail preserved from compressed AC list:
- Given approval action is disabled by blocker/RBAC, UI shows exact blocker/permission reason.
- Given closeout runs, screenshots and Playwright trace artifacts are captured.
- UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- app/(app)/technical/boms/[id]/_components/release-bundle-panel.tsx [create]
- app/(app)/technical/factory-specs/[id]/_components/release-bundle-panel.tsx [create]
- tests/ui/technical-release-bundle-panel.test.tsx [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the UI surface is implemented, when compared with the dedicated layout-primitive prototype `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/technical/spec-driven-screens.jsx:653-781` (`factory_spec_bom_bundle_approval_modal` paired-status grid, blockers/preflight panel, approval/rejection history list, approve/reject radio with disabled-on-blocker behaviour and clone-on-write banner), then it satisfies structural parity, visual parity, and interaction parity against the PRD §0/§5/§7.4 / UX §0A FactorySpec+BOM bundle approval layout/flow (PRD/UX is canonical, prototype is layout-primitive only); the panel surfaces paired factory_spec/BOM statuses, RM-usability/supplier_specs/release-guard blockers, clone-on-write banner and approval/rejection history, captures Playwright screenshots and traces, passes axe checks, and applies the Technical red-line overlay: FG not FA, WIP/intermediate not PR-code, factory_spec/internal_product_spec, shared BOM SSOT, and D365 optional only (sync is informational, never blocks Technical approval).
2. Given panel renders, paired factory_spec/BOM statuses, blockers, clone-on-write warning and approval history are visible.
3. Given D365 is disabled, UI explains local Technical approval still unlocks factory use.
4. Given the task is closed out, when reviewers inspect the task evidence, then these additional requirements are proven together: Given approval action is disabled by blocker/RBAC, UI shows exact blocker/permission reason.; Given closeout runs, screenshots and Playwright trace artifacts are captured.; UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.

## Test strategy
- RED UI tests for panel blockers, disabled D365 state, RBAC and prototype parity/red-line copy.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/ui/technical-release-bundle-panel.test.tsx
- For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.

## Risk red lines
- Do not implement API internals here; consume T-080.
- Do not claim NPD G4 alone unlocks factory use.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
- Do not paste prototype JSX verbatim — translate to production shadcn primitives per `_meta/prototype-labels/translation-notes-technical.md`.
- Do not use raw HTML <select> — use shadcn Select.
- Do not use inline styles — use Tailwind classes; conditional via cn()/cva().
- Do not import @radix-ui/* outside packages/ui (ESLint guard).

## Prototype parity
- Canonical anchor: `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/technical/spec-driven-screens.jsx:653-781` (factory_spec_bom_bundle_approval_modal) — see `_meta/prototype-labels/prototype-index-technical.json` entry and `_meta/prototype-labels/translation-notes-technical.md` for shadcn translation guidance.
- Evidence policy: `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md` — closeout must include screenshot artifacts + Playwright trace/video where applicable, plus axe results or documented blocker.
